name: Nava del Rey from Carpio – circular via Medina del Campo and the río Trabancos
name_original: Nava del Rey desde Carpio – circular por Medina del Campo y el río Trabancos
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Carpio traverse a landscape characterized by extensive agricultural plains and gentle undulations, typical of the Castilian plateau. The region features long, open roads that connect historic towns and villages, often following river valleys like the río Trabancos. Cyclists can expect a mix of flat sections and mild ascents, making it suitable for various fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Carpio The most popular road cycling route is Nava del Rey from Carpio – circular via Medina del Campo and the río Trabancos, a 41.1 miles (66.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete. This route offers a scenic loop through agricultural land and along the río Trabancos. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Roadbike loop from Cantalapiedra, a moderate 40.9 miles (65.9 km) path. This route features a mix of open plains and gentle climbs, providing varied terrain for an engaging ride. Local road cyclists also love the Palacio de Dueñas Courtyard – Town center of Nava del Rey loop from Nueva Villa de las Torres, a 35.2 miles (56.6 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and past historic towns, often completed in about 2 hours 27 minutes. Road cycling around Carpio is defined by open plains, agricultural fields, and river valleys, offering a tranquil cycling experience. The network provides options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more moderate challenges. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 50 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Carpio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Carpio?
A: There are 11 road cycling routes around Carpio listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences across the Castilian plateau. These routes traverse extensive agricultural plains and gentle undulations, connecting historic towns and villages.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Carpio?
A: Road cycling routes around Carpio are characterized by extensive agricultural plains and gentle undulations, typical of the Castilian plateau. You'll find long, open roads that often follow river valleys like the río Trabancos, with a mix of flat sections and mild ascents.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Carpio?
A: Yes, Carpio offers routes suitable for various fitness levels. While most routes are rated moderate, there is at least one easy route. The region's gentle undulations and mix of flat sections make it accessible for those new to road cycling or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in Carpio?
A: Yes, many routes around Carpio offer substantial distances. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175764060/nava-del-rey-from-carpio-circular-via-medina-del-campo-and-the-rio-trabancos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nava del Rey from Carpio – circular via Medina del Campo and the río Trabancos&lt;/a&gt; route is over 41 miles (66 km) long, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142894" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roadbike loop from Cantalapiedra&lt;/a&gt; is also around 40 miles (65 km), providing excellent options for longer rides.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Carpio?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular option in Carpio. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175764060/nava-del-rey-from-carpio-circular-via-medina-del-campo-and-the-rio-trabancos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nava del Rey from Carpio – circular via Medina del Campo and the río Trabancos&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic loop through agricultural land and along the río Trabancos. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31468341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palacio de Dueñas Courtyard – Town center of Nava del Rey loop from Nueva Villa de las Torres&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some popular road cycling routes in Carpio?
A: Among the most popular routes are the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175764060/nava-del-rey-from-carpio-circular-via-medina-del-campo-and-the-rio-trabancos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nava del Rey from Carpio – circular via Medina del Campo and the río Trabancos&lt;/a&gt;, known for its scenic loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142894" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roadbike loop from Cantalapiedra&lt;/a&gt;, which offers varied terrain with open plains and gentle climbs.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions along the road cycling routes in Carpio?
A: Yes, the routes often connect historic towns and villages, allowing you to explore local heritage. You might pass by landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5632626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saints John of Nava del Rey&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5768356" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walls of Madrigal de las Altas Torres&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5856875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Madrigal de las Altas Torres Historic Center&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31468341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palacio de Dueñas Courtyard – Town center of Nava del Rey loop from Nueva Villa de las Torres&lt;/a&gt; specifically leads past historic towns.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Carpio?
A: Given its location on the Castilian plateau, Carpio likely experiences a continental climate with warm summers and cold winters. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, with comfortable conditions for exploring the open plains and river valleys.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Carpio?
A: Road cyclists often appreciate the tranquil experience offered by Carpio's open plains, agricultural fields, and river valleys. The region provides a network of routes suitable for different ability levels, from easy rides to more moderate challenges, making it appealing for varied cycling preferences.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest to look out for while cycling?
A: While cycling, you can look out for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5457313" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Town center of Nava del Rey&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5783329" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María Church in Alaejos&lt;/a&gt;, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5768356" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walls of Madrigal de las Altas Torres&lt;/a&gt;. These offer glimpses into the region's rich history and culture amidst the scenic agricultural landscapes.

Q: What is the longest road cycling route available in Carpio?
A: The longest route currently listed is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175764060/nava-del-rey-from-carpio-circular-via-medina-del-campo-and-the-rio-trabancos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nava del Rey from Carpio – circular via Medina del Campo and the río Trabancos&lt;/a&gt;, spanning approximately 41.1 miles (66.2 km). This route offers a comprehensive tour through the region's characteristic landscapes.
tips: Valladolid town surrounded by dryland agriculture although in the past it had been dedicated to vineyards.

The medieval Palacio de las Dueñas is an interesting architectural monument with a well-preserved patio that surrounds it with a cloister and typical columns of the time. Today it is used as a high school, not like the schools I went to.

The Zapardiel River runs through the town of Medina del Campo on its way to the Duero River downstream. The river is born in the sierra de Ávila. River levels depend on the amount of rainfall, and actually the channel the river passes through is dry today.
